tldr: Iran has threatened to halt all Middle East energy exports and block trade routes, following renewed US strikes and a blockade.
Major US stock indices closed lower and volatility increased, influenced by these escalating international tensions despite a surprise drop in June's producer prices.
New tech developments include Samsung's more durable foldable displays, Motorola's first Android phone with 25W Qi2 wireless charging, and SpaceX's smaller, more efficient Starlink V5 dish.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closed lower on July 13, experiencing declines as renewed geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the US raised market uncertainty, overshadowing news of an unexpected fall in US producer prices for June. Iran has threatened to halt all Middle East energy exports and block trade routes amidst US strikes and a blockade, which likely contributed to investor apprehension.

yield_10y.why: The 10-year Treasury yield slightly increased to 4.61% as of July 13. This move came as traders assessed mixed signals, weighing an unexpected decline in producer price inflation against a potential oil rebound and renewed hostilities between the US and Iran, which could influence future inflation expectations.

vix.why: The VIX rose to 17.16 as of July 13, indicating increased market volatility and investor uncertainty. This heightened apprehension is likely a reaction to the escalating tensions in the Middle East, with Iran threatening to disrupt energy exports and block trade routes in response to US actions, creating a climate of geopolitical risk.

tech.summary: Samsung has introduced new "Flex Titanium" flexible display technology for its foldable phones, designed to be slimmer, more durable, and less prone to creasing.
Motorola has launched the Edge 70 Max, which is reportedly the first Android phone to support 25W Qi2 wireless charging, alongside a large battery and rapid wired charging.
SpaceX has made its Starlink V5 residential dish available in select areas; this latest version is notably smaller, lighter, and more power-efficient than its predecessor.

world.summary: Iran has threatened to halt all energy exports from the Middle East and block more trade routes, following renewed US strikes and a blockade, escalating tensions in the region.
Lithuania has issued warnings about potential Russian "targeted" attacks on critical infrastructure, though the Kremlin has dismissed these claims as "horror stories."
Extreme heat warnings are in effect for broad areas of the United States, as an unusual "heat dome" phenomenon has caused record-high temperatures across parts of the country.
